channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado, Farnaz Fassihi, Elian Peltier and Ephrat Livni

item.title: Hegseth Likens Easter Rescue of U.S. Airman to Resurrection of Jesus Christ
item.description: President Trump also asserted that God supports the American war against Iran “because God is good, and God wants to see people taken care of.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaking at a news conference at the White House on Monday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in Iran and the Middle East on Monday
item.description: President Trump escalated his threats ahead of his pending Tuesday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Inspecting damages on Monday inside a library of an ultra-Orthodox girls’ school following a missile strike in Tel Aviv.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Proposal Isn’t Enough to Stop Attacks on Bridges and Power Plants
item.description: President Trump has told Iran it must open the Strait of Hormuz by 8 p.m. Tuesday or face the consequences, although he has delayed previous deadlines.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Damaged buildings in Tehran. President Trump said on Monday that the United States would consider helping to rebuild Iran if they can strike a deal.

item.title: 6 Takeaways From Trump’s News Conference on Iran
item.description: President Trump described the risky mission to rescue an Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down, but he offered no clear path out of the war.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: “We have to have a deal that’s acceptable to me,” President Trump said on Monday. “And part of that deal is going to be we want free traffic of oil and everything else.”

item.title: Iran’s 10-Point Proposal Demands an End to Attacks and Sanctions
item.description: As President Trump’s deadline for new attacks loomed, Iran conveyed its conditions through Pakistani intermediaries.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Farnaz Fassihi, Shirin Hakim and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran shows Iranian military commanders with American aircraft in a net.

item.title: As Trump Deadline Looms, Iran and U.S. Mix Threats With Hints of Deal
item.description: One potential off-ramp appeared when Iran offered a 10-point counterproposal for ending the war that President Trump called a significant step, if “not good enough.”
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Erika Solomon, Aaron Boxerman and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A portrait of Iran’s late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Israel Steps Up Attacks on Lebanon, Killing Dozens
item.description: Sunday was a particularly deadly day, as Israel targeted areas around the country — including a Christian area long regarded as safe by residents.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Sarah Chaayto
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A neighborhood in Beirut on Monday, after an Israeli airstrike on Sunday.

item.title: Mapping the scale of the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Martín González Gómez

item.title: Iranians Condemn Strike on a Top University
item.description: Government officials and anti-government activists alike denounced the attacks on the Sharif University of Technology in Tehran, the latest Iranian center for higher education to be targeted.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Workers clear debris on Monday at Sharif University of Technology after it was struck overnight.

item.title: Trump’s Board of Peace Gives Hamas Disarmament Deadline
item.description: The demand reflects both the U.S. administration’s eagerness to secure a lasting cease-fire in Gaza and its growing impatience with the Palestinian militant group.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Natan Odenheimer and Abu Bakr Bashir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Armed members of Hamas in Khan Younis, Gaza, last year.

item.title: Key islands give Iran sway over the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Josh Holder and Adina Renner

item.title: Trump Moves Deadline for Iran to Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The new deadline comes as the president and Iranian leaders have ramped up bombastic threats against one another.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump has changed deadlines during the course of the Iran war.

item.title: At Least 4 Killed in Iranian Strike in Haifa, Israeli Officials Say
item.description: Rescue workers recovered four bodies from a residential building in the port city of Haifa after air defenses failed to intercept an Iranian strike, Israeli officials said.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency workers searching for missing people in the rubble of a residential building struck by a missile in Haifa, Israel, on Sunday.

item.title: The Latest Blows to Iran’s Leadership
item.description: An Iranian intelligence chief killed overnight on Monday was one of several Iranian officials who occupied their posts for only a few months.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Seyed Majid Khademi, Iranian Intelligence Chief, Killed in Overnight Attack
item.description: Israel claimed responsibility for the death of Major General Seyed Majid Khademi, the spy chief of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, the latest senior official to be killed in the war.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Office of the Iranian Supreme Leader, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A handout photograph released by the Office of the Iranian Supreme Leader showing Major General Seyed Majid Khademi, who was killed on Monday.

item.title: On Iran, Trump Keeps World Off Balance With Ever-Changing Threats
item.description: Global leaders are struggling in their efforts to find a way to end the American-Israeli war on Iran, and they are spooked about what President Trump might do next.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump has threatened to bomb crucial civilian infrastructure in Iran if Tehran does not agree to his demands by Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Erika Solomon and Richard Pérez-Peña

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: President Trump taunted Iran on social media, while strikes continued in Iran, Israel, Lebanon and some Gulf states.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency medical workers search through the rubble of a residential building struck by an Iranian missile attack on Sunday in Haifa, Israel.

item.title: Trump’s Lesson From Risky Rescue: Threaten to Go Harder at Iran
item.description: In an expletive-laced social media post, the president said Iran should open the Strait of Hormuz or he would bomb bridges and power plants.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: While President Trump still suggests that Iran is begging for a deal, intelligence analysis generated by the United States and some of its closest Western allies suggests the opposite.

item.title: Here’s a Timeline of Trump’s Ultimatums Over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump first gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the vital oil shipping route on March 21. The deadline has been reset many times since.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
item.media:description: Boats in the Strait of Hormuz in March. Transit through the waterway has slowed to a trickle since the start of the conflict.

item.title: Trump Revels in Threats to Commit War Crimes in Iran
item.description: The president said he would bomb Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” Until this administration, American leaders had insisted they were trying to follow international law in war.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump wrote on social media that “all Hell will reign down” on Iran unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: How the CIA Helped Locate a Missing Airman After Iran Downed a U.S. Fighter Jet
item.description: The intelligence agency also executed a deception campaign aimed at drawing Iranian forces away from where the missing Air Force officer was hiding.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A still image from a video of U.S. aircraft over southwest Iran on Friday.

item.title: Trump Seems Emboldened With New Threats in Truth Social Post Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: In an expletive-filled social media post, Mr. Trump said Iran should open the Strait of Hormuz or he will bomb bridges and power plants.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House last week.

item.title: Iran’s Downing of Fighter Jet and U.S. Rescue Leave Both Sides Dangerously Emboldened
item.description: After Iran shot down a U.S. plane and U.S. forces pulled off a risky ground operation to extricate a stranded airman, both sides claimed victory. That confidence could fuel further escalation.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An anti-U.S. billboard on Sunday in Tehran showing American aircraft captured in a net.

item.title: Interceptor Missiles Save Lives, but Stockpiles Are Dwindling
item.description: Defense systems that shoot projectiles out of the sky have become one of the most important components of warfare, so much so that militaries face a diminishing supply.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Manama, Bahrain, after a drone was intercepted near the Bahrain Financial Harbour towers last month.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Greg Jaffe, Helene Cooper, Eric Schmitt and David E. Sanger

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: The U.S. military’s search-and-rescue operation for a missing American airman entered its second day. The Israeli military struck a major petrochemical complex in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The site of a strike at Shahid Beheshti University in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Israel Strikes Iran’s Largest Petrochemical Complex
item.description: The sprawling complex in the city of Mahshahr was taken offline after strikes hit two utility plants that provided basic services like gas and power, Iranian officials said.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Mahshahr petrochemical complex in 2011.

item.title: Satellite image company restricts Mideast content, citing a U.S. government request.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:credit: Planet Labs PBC, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A satellite photo of Kharg Island in Iran in February that was distributed by Planet Labs.

item.title: Here’s how two airmen would have ejected from the F-15E.
item.description: A step-by-step look at the process by which the two crew members probably ejected from the fighter jet.
item.dc:creator: Mika Gröndahl, Lily Boyce and Thomas Gibbons-Neff

item.title: U.S. Fighter Jet Downed Over Iran Was Probably Based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, U.K. Airfield
item.description: A military analyst identified markings consistent with a squadron based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, one of two British bases that host the largest U.S. fighter jet operation in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A fighter jet at R.A.F. Lakenheath in Mildenhall, England, in January.

item.title: A projectile hit near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing a worker.
item.description: There was no reported increase in radiation levels. A string of recent attacks near the Persian Gulf facility has fueled fears of a nuclear accident.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.media:description: A satellite image of the nuclear power plant in Bushehr, Iran, in December.

item.title: Missing Airman Raises Concerns That Iran Could Gain Leverage Over the U.S.
item.description: Since 1979, Iran has repeatedly used Americans and Europeans detained on its territory to win concessions over more powerful adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Women walking by a mural of the Statue of Liberty on the outer wall of the former U.S. Embassy in Tehran. In 1979, a group of militant students took over the embassy and kept 52 Americans captive for 444 days.

item.title: Trump Pledged a Quick End to the Iran War, but He Hasn’t Explained How
item.description: President Trump bet that American firepower could cow Iran into compliance. So far, Iran’s leaders have been unwilling to quit.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Neither President Trump nor Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has been able to explain how the war, now in its fifth week, will end.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Eric Schmitt and Helene Cooper

item.title: ‘Iran Is Being Destroyed in Front of Our Eyes’: Tehran Is Gripped by Fear
item.description: Fifteen residents of Tehran said in telephone interviews and text messages that the capital was weathering heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Residents in front of buildings damaged by airstrikes in Karaj, Iran, on Friday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Friday
item.description: An American fighter jet was shot down by Iran for the first time since the start of the war, leading to an urgent search-and-rescue operation for a missing crew member.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: A home in Ramat Gan, Israel, damaged by a projectile during an Iranian missile strike on Friday.

item.title: Ex-US Army Pilot Recalls Battle for Survival When Shot Down in Iraq
item.description: Ronald Young Jr. was in the Army when his Apache Longbow copter went down during the U.S. invasion of Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ronald Young Jr. boarding a transport plane near Baghdad in 2003, after escaping captivity.

item.title: Iran’s Defenses Have Been Struck, but They Can Still Fire Missiles and Drones
item.description: Iran has sent waves of missiles and drones at Israel and Gulf states in recent weeks and shot down an American fighter jet on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Planet Labs Pbc/Planet Labs PBC, via Associated Press
item.media:description: This satellite photo shows damage after an Iranian attack at Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar last month. Iran struck the regional air headquarters of U.S. Central Command, damaging an early-warning radar system.

item.title: Iran Shoots Down U.S. Fighter Jet, Setting Off American Search and Rescue
item.description: One crew member was rescued. The risky operation to find the second airman came as the United States and Israel struck infrastructure targets in Iran, prompting Iranian retaliation in the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Thomas Fuller
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: What We Know About the Rescue of a U.S. Airman After Two Jet Crashes
item.description: An airman was rescued on Saturday night, President Trump said. The rescue came after a risky two-day operation deep inside Iran.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor, Aurelien Breeden and Yan Zhuang
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An F-15E Strike Eagle, the type of plane shot down in Iran.

item.title: Iran Is Quickly Repairing Missile Bunkers, U.S. Intelligence Says
item.description: Reports cast doubt on how close the United States is to destroying Iran’s missile capability, a key goal in the war.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The tail section of a missile in Israel’s northern Jordan Valley on Friday. Iran has continued to strike at the country, even with its reduced arsenal and more careful use of launchers.

item.title: The F-15E Strike Eagle: What to Know
item.description: The U.S. military aircraft with a two-person crew was shot down in Iran on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The U.S. aircraft that was brought down over Iran has been identified as an F-15E Strike Eagle. One of its two-member crew is still missing.

item.title: Hegseth Says U.S. Troops Are Fighting for Jesus. The Pope Disagrees.
item.description: In sharp contrast to the Trump administration’s calls for Christian prayers for the war effort, Pope Leo XIV says military domination is “entirely foreign to the way of Jesus Christ.”
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Pope Leo, during the Holy Thursday Mass in Rome, said the Christian mission had often been “distorted by a desire for domination.”

item.title: What U.S. military Pilots Are Trained to Do if Shot Down
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: American military pilots are trained in a set of principles called Survival, Evasion, Resistance and Escape to prepare for the possibility of being shot down in hostile territory.
